scenario: postgres pgsql-hackers mailing list — jsonb subscript debate
description: Synthetic archived messages. The harvester must build an open graph: people, patches, code files, threads, and the free-text relationships between them. Messages 1001 and 1002 restate the same Tom Lane → patch relationship (one via the alias 'tgl') so the eval can verify dedup + edge reinforcement. Message 1003 introduces a second person (Andres Freund) and a review relationship.

messages.subject: Re: jsonb subscripting semantics (v3)
messages.body: I looked over the v3 fix jsonb subscript patch. It touches src/backend/utils/adt/jsonbsubs.c and changes how a missing key is handled. This revives the argument from the 2025 jsonb subscript semantics thread — we never settled whether assignment to a missing path should auto-vivify. I comment on the patch with that concern. — tgl

messages.subject: Re: jsonb subscripting semantics (v3)
messages.body: Following up on my earlier note: I still comment on the v3 fix jsonb subscript patch. The change in src/backend/utils/adt/jsonbsubs.c is the right shape, but the error message wording needs work. Same patch, same file.

messages.subject: Re: jsonb subscripting semantics (v3)
messages.body: I reviews the v3 fix jsonb subscript patch from the performance angle. I disagrees with Tom Lane about auto-vivification — I think the extra branch in src/backend/utils/adt/jsonbsubs.c is cheap enough. Otherwise the patch looks good to me.
